description_long: This object is a single-piece crutch with a gently curved shaft and a metallic finish at both ends. The main shaft is slender and elongated, featuring a smooth, slightly reflective surface with a warm, orange-brown coloration. The upper end of the crutch is capped with a broad, flat, and slightly angled section, which serves as the underarm support, while the lower end terminates in a flattened, wedge-like tip for ground contact. The crutch lacks any visible padding, forearm support, or adjustment mechanisms, suggesting a fixed-length design. Its overall form is streamlined and utilitarian, with no additional ergonomic or comfort features apparent. The design is reminiscent of a traditional walking aid, suitable for providing support and stability on urban surfaces.
description: A streamlined, fixed-length crutch with a curved shaft, metallic ends, and a wedge-like tip. The design is simple and utilitarian, lacking padding or adjustability.
description_view_0: The crutch is seen from the narrow edge, highlighting the flat, broad underarm support and the slim profile of the shaft.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the gentle curve of the shaft and the angled underarm support, as well as the wedge-shaped tip.
description_view_2: This rear view shows the continuous, slender shaft and the flat, broad surfaces at both ends.
description_view_3: Another side view, mirroring view 1, emphasizes the curvature and the distinct shapes of the upper and lower ends.
attribute_tip_design: flat, wedge-like tip for ground contact
